content.method: Legal analysis by constitutional and EU law experts examining precedent cases on conditional public funding tied to demographic outcomes; comparative review of similar fiscal-conditionality mechanisms in other EU member states' education systems and their legal survival under challenge.
content.question: Is fiscal conditionality (tying funding premiums to demographic composition/equity metrics) legally permissible under Hungary's constitutional framework and EU non-discrimination law, and can it be enforced against church, private, and municipal maintainers who retain admissions autonomy?
content.why_it_matters: T6's entire mechanism depends on legal feasibility that is currently unaddressed in the evidence record; without resolving this, T6 cannot be meaningfully compared to other proposals on cost-effectiveness or political feasibility grounds, since it may not be implementable at all.
